2026-06-26.title: Twin Earthquakes Leave 235 Dead in Venezuela
2026-06-26.facts: Venezuela is racing against time to find survivors after two powerful earthquakes struck the country, killing at least 235 people and leaving hundreds more missing or trapped under rubble. Thousands were injured in the quakes, which devastated coastal areas including the port city of La Guaira, where multi-storey buildings collapsed.

2026-06-26.title: Ransomware Attack on Jaguar Land Rover Cost UK Economy $2.5 Billion
2026-06-26.facts: A massive cyberattack that crippled Jaguar Land Rover last year has been traced to Russian operatives, costing the British economy an estimated $2.5 billion. Investigators initially believed a loose collective of cybercriminals was behind the ransomware attack, which shut down production lines and disrupted supply chains across the automotive giant.

2026-06-26.title: Bolton Expected to Plead Guilty in Classified Documents Case
2026-06-26.facts: John Bolton is expected to plead guilty to mishandling classified information, potentially facing prison time in a case that stretched across two administrations. The former national security adviser to President Trump will admit to the charges in what marks a rare criminal resolution for a figure at his level of government.

2026-06-26.title: Supreme Court Backs End to Deportation Protections
2026-06-26.facts: The Supreme Court ruled that the Trump administration can end deportation protections for certain migrants, handing immigration hard-liners a victory after a decade of legal battles. A White House official called it "a victory 10 years in the making" following years of courtroom losses before reaching the high court.

2026-06-26.title: California Ballot Fight Erupts Over Billionaire Tax
2026-06-26.facts: California voters will decide on a billionaire tax this November, but the state's wealthiest residents are mounting a fierce counterattack. The wealthy have placed two competing ballot measures designed to nullify the tax before it can take effect.

2026-06-26.title: Dollar Surges to Year High Despite Gulf Peace Deal
2026-06-26.facts: The U.S. dollar has climbed to its highest point of the year, defying expectations that a peace agreement ending the Gulf conflict would weaken demand for safe-haven assets.

2026-06-26.title: Tech Giants Partner with Government on Worker A.I. Transition
2026-06-26.facts: OpenAI, Anthropic, Amazon, and Microsoft have joined a new initiative aimed at easing the impact of artificial intelligence on American workers. The effort is being led by former Commerce Secretary Gina Raimondo, bringing together some of the biggest players in A.I.

2026-06-26.title: Asian Markets Tumble as Tech Selloff Intensifies
2026-06-26.facts: Stock markets across Asia slid sharply as a rout in technology shares deepened, with South Korea's Kospi index hit so hard that trading was halted for the third time this week. Circuit breakers kicked in to prevent panic selling as investors dumped tech stocks across the region.

2026-06-26.title: Russell Rebalancing Set to Shake Markets Friday
2026-06-26.facts: Wall Street's about to see one of its busiest trading days of the year, and it's got nothing to do with earnings or the Fed. FTSE Russell is conducting its semi-annual rebalancing—what the index provider calls reconstitution—and analysts say the sheer size of the operation could move markets Friday.

2026-06-26.title: NYC Rent Freeze Approved for Nearly One Million Apartments
2026-06-26.facts: The Rent Guidelines Board just voted to freeze rents on nearly one million rent-stabilized apartments across New York City, marking the first time in years that tenants will see zero increase when their leases renew. It's a campaign promise fulfilled for the mayor, who pushed hard for the freeze amid persistent affordability concerns.

2026-06-26.title: Long Island Real Estate Scheme Leaves Buyers in Legal Nightmare
2026-06-26.facts: A Long Island deed scheme has left multiple homebuyers trapped in what's being described as a never-ending real estate nightmare. Michael O'Sullivan allegedly promised clients their dream homes but instead delivered a tangle of fraudulent paperwork and legal troubles.

2026-06-26.title: Baseball Owners Push Contract Cap in Labor Talks
2026-06-26.facts: Major League Baseball is pushing for a five-year cap on free agent contracts for players changing teams, according to details emerging from ongoing collective bargaining talks. Under the proposal, teams could only sign another team's free agent to a maximum of five years, though they'd be allowed to keep their own players for up to six years under what's being called a Cornerstone Player Provision.

2026-06-26.title: LaMelo Ball Traded to Minnesota in Blockbuster Deal
2026-06-26.facts: The Hornets are sending LaMelo Ball and Josh Green to the Timberwolves in exchange for Naz Reid and a series of draft picks. It's one of the biggest moves of the NBA offseason, reshaping both franchises heading into next season.

2026-06-26.title: Anthropic Accuses Alibaba of Systematic Campaign to Clone Claude
2026-06-26.facts: Anthropic is accusing Alibaba of running what it calls a brazen, systematic campaign to illegally extract the capabilities of its Claude AI model. The company says Alibaba used what's known as model distillation—essentially querying Claude millions of times to reverse-engineer how it works and replicate that intelligence in Alibaba's own competing models.

2026-06-27.title: Devastating Earthquakes Kill Hundreds in Venezuela
2026-06-27.facts: At least 920 people are dead and more than 51,000 are missing after two massive earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday. International rescue teams have arrived as desperate families dig through rubble, but the critical 72-hour rescue window is nearly closed.

2026-06-27.title: Italy's Meloni and Trump Relationship Collapses
2026-06-27.facts: The political alliance between Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and former President Trump has deteriorated from close partnership to open hostility. Meloni was once dubbed the "Trump whisperer" for her ability to navigate the relationship, but that's now completely unraveled.

2026-06-27.title: Exiled Myanmar Chefs Bring Burmese Cuisine to Global Spotlight
2026-06-27.facts: Burmese food is finally getting international recognition, thanks to an unlikely catalyst: political exile. An exodus of chefs from Myanmar following the military coup has spread the country's cuisine to new corners of the world.

2026-06-27.title: U.S. and Iran Exchange Strikes as Truce Unravels
2026-06-27.facts: The fragile U.S.-Iran agreement is teetering after a new round of military strikes overnight. The United States hit Iranian military sites after a cargo ship came under attack in the Strait of Hormuz for the second time in recent days.

2026-06-27.title: Alaska Senate Race Gets Confusing as Two Dan Sullivans Approved for Ballot
2026-06-27.facts: A judge in Alaska has ruled that two candidates named Dan Sullivan can both appear on the ballot for the state's U.S. Senate race.

2026-06-27.title: Colorado Redistricting Proposals in Limbo as Supreme Court Stays Silent
2026-06-27.facts: Colorado's highest court is sitting on a decision that could determine whether Democrats get a shot at redrawing the state's electoral maps before 2028. Both parties are waiting for the state Supreme Court to rule on whether ballot proposals to redistrict Colorado are legally valid.

2026-06-27.title: Louisiana Republicans Head to Senate Runoff in Test of Trump's Sway
2026-06-27.facts: Louisiana voters are heading to the polls Saturday for a Republican Senate runoff that'll offer fresh evidence of how much clout President Trump still holds in GOP primaries. The race is the latest in a string of intraparty contests where Trump's endorsement has been a central factor.

2026-06-27.title: Trump Threatens 100% Tariff on Europe Over Tech Taxes
2026-06-27.facts: President Trump is threatening to slap a 100% tariff on European countries that impose digital services taxes on American tech companies. The move would override a trade agreement with the European Union that was finalized just days ago.

2026-06-27.title: American Consumer Confidence Rebounds After Energy Price Surge
2026-06-27.facts: Americans are feeling noticeably better about the economy, according to a new consumer sentiment survey. Confidence rose in recent weeks after plummeting earlier this year when energy costs soared.

2026-06-27.title: Brexit's Labor Crunch Brings Central Asian Workers to British Farms
2026-06-27.facts: A decade after Brexit, Britain's strawberry fields are being picked by workers from Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan—not Europeans. Most seasonal agricultural laborers now come from Central Asia, a dramatic shift from the pre-Brexit era when EU citizens dominated the workforce.

2026-06-27.title: Tech Stocks Stumble as AI Spending Questions Mount
2026-06-27.facts: Tech stocks just suffered one of their worst weeks in a year, and it's all about a question Wall Street had been avoiding: what are we actually getting for all this AI spending? The sell-off hit software companies particularly hard, though there's an interesting split emerging.

2026-06-27.title: NYC Rent Freeze Hits Nearly One Million Apartments
2026-06-27.facts: New York City's Rent Guidelines Board just voted to freeze rents on nearly one million rent-stabilized apartments, delivering on a key campaign promise from Mayor Mamdani. It's the first time in years tenants in these units won't see an increase when their leases renew.

2026-06-27.title: Long Island Real Estate Fraud Leaves Homebuyers Stranded
2026-06-27.facts: A real estate scheme on Long Island has left multiple families in a legal and financial nightmare after they thought they'd purchased their dream homes. Michael O'Sullivan allegedly promised clients straightforward home purchases, but what they got instead were tangled deed transfers that never properly completed.

2026-06-27.title: Angels Clean House, Bring in Cardinals Architect for Overhaul
2026-06-27.facts: The Angels fired general manager Perry Minasian on Friday and immediately announced they're bringing in John Mozeliak as a consultant to rebuild their baseball operations department. Mozeliak spent years as a top executive with the Cardinals, and the Angels are clearly hoping he can work the same magic in Anaheim.

2026-06-27.title: Thunder Lock Up Hartenstein With $75 Million Extension
2026-06-27.facts: Isaiah Hartenstein is staying in Oklahoma City. The center intends to sign a new three-year contract worth $75 million to return to the Thunder through the 2029 season.

2026-06-27.title: Anthropic Wins U.S. Approval to Release Mythos AI Model Under Tight Controls
2026-06-27.facts: The U.S. government just gave Anthropic the green light to release its most powerful AI model yet—Claude Mythos—but only to a select group of over 100 American companies and government agencies.

2026-06-28.title: Venezuela Struggles With Earthquake Response Amid Political Turmoil
2026-06-28.facts: Desperate families in Venezuela are keeping vigil outside collapsed buildings, calling out to loved ones they fear are trapped beneath the rubble. The country's interim president, Delcy Rodríguez, is facing accusations from critics that she's exploiting the earthquake tragedy for political gain.

2026-06-28.title: Record-Breaking Heatwave Scorches Northern Europe
2026-06-28.facts: An estimated 150 million people across Europe are sweltering through temperatures above 35 degrees Celsius as a heatwave shatters records in Germany, Denmark, and the Czech Republic. The extreme heat has broken longstanding temperature benchmarks in all three countries, pushing northern European infrastructure and populations unaccustomed to such sustained warmth to their limits.

2026-06-28.title: Pilot Killed After Crashing Small Plane Into Beijing's Tallest Tower
2026-06-28.facts: China has confirmed the death of a pilot who flew a small plane into Beijing's tallest tower, an incident that also injured 13 others. The crash occurred just five miles from Communist Party headquarters, sending debris and plane parts falling to the ground below.

2026-06-28.title: Iran and U.S. Exchange Strikes as Tensions Enter Fourth Day
2026-06-28.facts: Hostilities between the United States and Iran have entered a fourth day with no signs of de-escalation, as both sides traded attacks and threats. Iran launched what it called retaliatory strikes at U.S.

2026-06-28.title: Michigan Parents Charged in Death of Seven-Year-Old Who Weighed 255 Pounds
2026-06-28.facts: A Michigan couple is facing murder charges after their seven-year-old son died weighing 255 pounds. Prosecutors say Casper O'Brien was morbidly obese, bedridden, and survived on little more than snack foods when he died last year.

2026-06-28.title: Colorado Governor's Race Tests Democratic Strategy on Trump
2026-06-28.facts: Colorado's Democratic primary on Tuesday is shaping up as a referendum on how to take on Trump. Senator Michael Bennet, once considered a lock for the nomination, is now in a tight race with Attorney General Phil Weiser.

2026-06-28.title: Alzheimer's Cost Crisis Looms Over U.S. Economy
2026-06-28.facts: There's a disease costing America more than cancer and heart disease combined, and it's about to get much worse. Health experts are sounding the alarm that the economic burden is reaching crisis levels for both families and government budgets.

2026-06-28.title: Medicare Opens Door to Weight-Loss Drugs
2026-06-28.facts: Starting July 1st, Medicare beneficiaries can access GLP-1 weight-loss medications for the first time—but it comes with important caveats. Qualifying seniors will be able to get these drugs for about $50 a month, a fraction of their usual retail price.

2026-06-28.title: Trump Threatens Europe With 100% Tariff Over Tech Tax
2026-06-28.facts: The U.S. president is threatening to slap a 100% tariff on European nations if they move forward with plans for a digital services tax.

2026-06-28.title: 401(k) Balances Hit All-Time Highs
2026-06-28.facts: Workplace retirement savers had their best year on record in 2024, according to Vanguard's latest report tracking how America saves. The investment giant's data shows 401(k) balances reached record levels across the board last year, driven by strong market returns and steady contributions.

2026-06-28.title: Historic Rent Freeze Hits New York Landlords
2026-06-28.facts: New York City's Rent Guidelines Board just voted to freeze rents on rent-stabilized apartments for the first time in its history—and it's not just one-year leases. Thursday night's 7-to-1 vote means both one- and two-year stabilized leases will see zero increase, a move that's unprecedented for the two-year category.

2026-06-28.title: Austria and Algeria Advance, Sending Iran Home in World Cup Drama
2026-06-28.facts: A wild 3-3 draw between Austria and Algeria on Saturday night sealed one of the World Cup's most unusual outcomes. Both teams traded goals in a thriller that sent them both through to the knockout round while eliminating Iran from the tournament entirely.

2026-06-28.title: Thunder Lock Up Hartenstein in $75 Million Deal
2026-06-28.facts: Isaiah Hartenstein is staying put in Oklahoma City. The Thunder center has agreed to a new three-year contract worth $75 million that'll keep him with the team through the 2028-29 season.

2026-06-28.title: Trump Administration Greenlights Anthropic's Mythos Model for Select U.S. Organizations
2026-06-28.facts: The Trump administration has authorized Anthropic to deploy its Mythos AI model to a limited set of U.S. companies and government agencies, ending a months-long review of the powerful system.

2026-06-29.title: Venezuela Races Against Time After Deadly Earthquake
2026-06-29.facts: Volunteers armed with drills, picks, and hammers are digging through concrete in a middle-class Caracas neighborhood, searching for survivors after a devastating earthquake struck Venezuela. The quake has triggered an extraordinary community response, with neighbors working around the clock to rescue those trapped beneath collapsed buildings.

2026-06-29.title: Russia Downplays War as Ukrainian Strikes Hit Home
2026-06-29.facts: Russian officials are avoiding the word "war" and refusing to designate bomb shelters or activate air raid sirens, even as Ukraine brings the conflict directly to Russian soil. It's a stark shift from the early days of the invasion—now, as Ukrainian drones and missiles strike deeper into Russian territory, Moscow is clinging to euphemisms and downplaying the danger to its own citizens.

2026-06-29.title: Pakistan Launches Deadly Airstrikes Into Afghanistan
2026-06-29.facts: Pakistani warplanes struck targets inside Afghanistan over the weekend, killing more than 30 people in the latest flare-up of a monthslong border conflict. The Taliban government says 36 civilians died in the airstrikes, while Pakistan insists it killed 32 militants in what it described as a targeted operation.

2026-06-29.title: U.S. and Iran Announce Deal to Ease Strait of Hormuz Tensions
2026-06-29.facts: The United States says it has reached an agreement with Iran to halt attacks and allow vessels to move freely through the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most critical oil chokepoints. A U.S.

2026-06-29.title: Three Firefighters Killed in Southwest Wildfires
2026-06-29.facts: Three firefighters have died battling wildfires that are tearing through Utah and Colorado this weekend. The fires are raging across the arid Southwest after what forecasters are calling an unusually warm winter.

2026-06-29.title: Supreme Court to Rule on Birthright Citizenship and Fed Independence
2026-06-29.facts: The Supreme Court will decide this week whether President Trump can end birthright citizenship guaranteed under the Constitution and fire the chair of the Federal Reserve. The two cases represent some of the most consequential decisions of the Court's term.

2026-06-29.title: Springfield's Haitian Immigration Boom Faces Uncertain Future
2026-06-29.facts: Springfield, Ohio is bracing for economic upheaval as a humanitarian program that brought thousands of Haitian immigrants to the city faces its end. The program revitalized what had been a struggling Rust Belt community, filling jobs and breathing new life into empty storefronts.

2026-06-29.title: Comcast Breaks Up NBCUniversal After 15 Years
2026-06-29.facts: Comcast announced Monday it's spinning off NBCUniversal into a separate company, unwinding a media marriage that's lasted 15 years. The cable giant acquired the entertainment and news business back in 2009, but now it's splitting the two apart entirely.

2026-06-29.title: Momentum Stocks Suffer Fourth-Worst Week in Two Decades
2026-06-29.facts: The so-called Magnificent Seven tech stocks took a beating last week, dragging momentum investing to its fourth-worst performance in 22 years. The S&P 500 underperformed its equally weighted counterpart by a hefty 350 basis points, meaning the handful of mega-cap stocks that drove gains for so long suddenly became a drag.

2026-06-29.title: Mortgage Shoppers Look Beyond Rates
2026-06-29.facts: Life events are now driving mortgage decisions more than interest rates, marking a shift in how Americans approach homebuying in 2026. Lenders say borrowers are increasingly motivated by personal milestones like marriage, job relocations, and growing families rather than waiting for rate drops.

2026-06-29.title: Boutique Properties Lure Luxury Buyers Back to Manhattan
2026-06-29.facts: Manhattan's luxury market is seeing a resurgence, with high-end buyers gravitating toward boutique condos and townhouses rather than glassy supertall towers. Developers and brokers report strong interest in smaller, character-rich buildings that offer exclusivity and architectural distinction.

2026-06-29.title: Red Sox Complete Stunning Yankees Sweep in Wild Extra-Inning Finish
2026-06-29.facts: The Red Sox capped a four-game sweep of the Yankees with a 5-4 victory in ten innings Sunday night at Fenway Park, rallying from what looked like certain defeat. Jarren Duran singled home the winning run to finish a three-run rally in the tenth inning.

2026-06-29.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo Traded to Heat in Blockbuster Deal
2026-06-29.facts: The Milwaukee Bucks have traded Giannis Antetokounmpo to the Miami Heat in what's shaping up as one of the biggest blockbuster deals in recent NBA history. The trade sends the two-time MVP and one of the league's most dominant players to South Florida in a move that reshapes the Eastern Conference landscape.

2026-06-29.title: Hornets Send Miles Bridges to Phoenix in Multi-Pick Swap
2026-06-29.facts: Charlotte's trading Miles Bridges to the Phoenix Suns in a deal that includes multiple draft picks and veteran players heading back to the Hornets. Phoenix is sending Grayson Allen and Royce O'Neale to Charlotte along with a 2033 first-round pick, while the Hornets are including a 2029 first-rounder and a second-round pick in the package.

2026-06-29.title: Anthropic Lands Major Reseller Deal in India
2026-06-29.facts: Hexaware Technologies' stock jumped eight percent after announcing a deal to resell Claude across India's enterprise market. The Mumbai-based IT services firm will embed Anthropic's models into client workflows, marking one of Claude's first major distribution partnerships outside North America and Europe.

2026-06-30.title: Twin Earthquakes Leave Venezuela Counting Unknown Dead
2026-06-30.facts: Five days after twin earthquakes leveled entire neighborhoods in Venezuela, the official death toll stands at 1,719—but experts warn that number could be a serious undercount. The difficulty in reaching accurate figures stems from the sheer scale of destruction across residential areas, where entire buildings collapsed and rescue efforts have been slow to reach remote zones.

2026-06-30.title: Bomb Targets Ukrainian Oligarch in Monaco
2026-06-30.facts: A manhunt is underway in Monaco after an explosive device injured a Ukrainian oligarch and several others in the tiny principality. The head of Monaco's government said the blast was caused by a bomb that appeared to contain bolts and pellets, suggesting it was designed to cause maximum harm.

2026-06-30.title: China Ramps Up Pressure Campaign Against Japan
2026-06-30.facts: Beijing is intensifying both military and economic pressure on Japan through a series of coordinated moves that include flying bombers near Japanese territory. China has also detained Japanese businesspeople operating in the country and imposed new restrictions on exports of rare earth minerals critical to Japan's tech industry.

2026-06-30.title: U.S. and Iran Head to Qatar for High-Stakes Talks
2026-06-30.facts: American and Iranian officials have arrived in Qatar for meetings that could determine whether recent efforts to broker a lasting peace deal survive. Qatar has served as the key mediator between Washington and Tehran, particularly after a new round of attacks last week threatened to derail months of diplomatic progress.

2026-06-30.title: Two Dead in Queens Bus Crash on Long Island Expressway
2026-06-30.facts: A bus flipped into oncoming traffic on the Long Island Expressway in Queens just before midnight Monday, killing two people. The crash involved multiple vehicles near 50th Street, shutting down lanes as authorities investigate what caused the bus to overturn.

2026-06-30.title: Businessman Who Courted U.S. Conservatives Sentenced for Fraud
2026-06-30.facts: A former Chinese billionaire who cultivated ties with American conservatives has been sentenced for fraud after using his supporters' money to fund a lavish lifestyle. The businessman, who worked closely with Steve Bannon, presented himself as an anti-Communist crusader while allegedly funneling donations into luxury real estate and a Bugatti supercar.

2026-06-30.title: Tight Race in Texas as Talarico and Paxton Tie in New Poll
2026-06-30.facts: Texas has a dead heat on its hands. A new New York Times and Siena College poll shows Democratic state lawmaker James Talarico tied with Republican Attorney General Ken Paxton in the state's Senate race.

2026-06-30.title: Strait of Hormuz Reopening Faster Than Expected
2026-06-30.facts: The Strait of Hormuz is reopening faster than anticipated following the war in Iran, and that's pushing oil prices down. Morgan Stanley's oil analysts cut their price targets for this year and next, citing the quicker-than-expected return to normal shipping operations through the critical waterway.

2026-06-30.title: Quant Funds Hit Hardest Trading Loss of the Year
2026-06-30.facts: Quantitative investment funds just endured their worst week of 2026 as momentum stocks suddenly collapsed. These computer-driven funds, which use algorithms to identify and ride market trends, got caught flat-footed when the momentum strategy they'd been riding simply stopped working.

2026-06-30.title: Chinese Tycoon Gets Thirty Years for Fraud
2026-06-30.facts: A Chinese businessman who styled himself as a dissident critic of Beijing just received a thirty-year prison sentence in the United States for running a massive fraud scheme. Guo Wengui built a following among Chinese expatriates and others by positioning himself as a government opponent and whistleblower.

2026-06-30.title: Gold Eyes Worst Quarter in Over a Decade
2026-06-30.facts: Gold is staring down its worst quarterly performance in thirteen years, even as it continues to flirt with the four-thousand-dollar mark. The precious metal has been locked in a volatile back-and-forth around that psychological threshold, unable to break through decisively.

2026-06-30.title: Brooklyn Tenants Sue Over Sewage and Mold Despite Ninety-Six Million in State Funds
2026-06-30.facts: Tenants at Rutland Plaza in Brownsville are taking their landlord to housing court over conditions they say have gone from bad to worse. The 438-unit complex has racked up more than two thousand open violations with the city's Housing Preservation and Development department, including black mold, broken elevators, and raw sewage in apartments.

2026-06-30.title: Germany Crash Out of World Cup in Stunning Penalty Shootout Loss
2026-06-30.facts: Paraguay knocked Germany out of the World Cup in a penalty shootout after a dramatic match that ended 1-1. Kai Havertz, Nick Woltemade, and Jonathan Tah all missed their spot kicks as Germany's tournament came to a shocking end.

2026-06-30.title: Memphis Ships Morant to Portland in Blockbuster Deal
2026-06-30.facts: The Memphis Grizzlies have traded two-time All-Star point guard Ja Morant to the Portland Trail Blazers in a deal that reshapes both franchises. Portland is sending Jerami Grant and Kris Murray back to Memphis in exchange for the explosive guard.

2026-06-30.title: California Agencies Get Half-Price Claude Access
2026-06-30.facts: California just became the first state to cut a sweeping deal with Anthropic, handing every state agency and local government a 50% discount on Claude. Governor Newsom announced the partnership today, framing it as a way to modernize public services without the usual procurement red tape.

2026-07-01.title: Dozens of Deported Venezuelans Missing After Earthquake
2026-07-01.facts: Many of the 146 Venezuelans deported from the United States the day a massive earthquake struck are now feared dead. The deportees arrived back in Venezuela just as the twin quakes devastated the country, and they've been missing or found dead in the days since.

2026-07-01.title: Fire Kills Several in Antwerp Apartment Tower
2026-07-01.facts: A fire tore through a ten-story apartment building in Antwerp's Linkeroever district, killing several people and injuring others. The blaze broke out in the Belgian city, though authorities haven't yet said how many victims there are or what started it.

2026-07-01.title: Plane Crashes Into Beijing Tower, China Silent on Details
2026-07-01.facts: A plane collided with a tower in Beijing, leaving visible holes in the side of the building, but Chinese authorities aren't saying what happened. The crash left damage clear enough that images circulated before Beijing scrubbed most traces of the incident from the public eye.

2026-07-01.title: Ukraine Expands Drone Campaign With Strike on Russian Oil Refinery
2026-07-01.facts: Ukraine hit another Russian oil refinery with a long-range drone, part of what President Zelenskyy says is an expanding campaign deep inside Russian territory. The strike targeted energy infrastructure far from the front lines, a tactic Ukraine's been using to squeeze Russia's fuel supplies and military logistics.

2026-07-01.title: Supreme Court Splits on Birthright Citizenship Order
2026-07-01.facts: The Supreme Court struck down President Trump's executive order on birthright citizenship, but the vote was closer than many expected. A bare majority of justices ruled the order unconstitutional, marking a significant conservative shift on an issue that once seemed settled.

2026-07-01.title: Dallas Launches Texas Stock Exchange as Wall Street Alternative
2026-07-01.facts: Dallas is officially launching the Texas Stock Exchange, aiming to build what boosters are calling "Y'all Street" as a rival to New York's financial dominance. City leaders are betting that a growing cluster of Wall Street firms relocating to Texas can cement Dallas as a major financial hub.

2026-07-01.title: Professor Wins Nearly Two Million in Firing Settlement
2026-07-01.facts: A former University of Tennessee professor has won a $1.9 million settlement after she was fired for criticizing Charlie Kirk on social media. Tamar Shirinian lost her job at the University of Tennessee at Knoxville over posts about the slain conservative activist.

2026-07-01.title: Trade Pact Renewal at a Crossroads
2026-07-01.facts: The United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement faces a critical moment as talks begin this week that could determine whether the trade pact survives. July 1 marks a potential turning point for the deal that replaced NAFTA, which President Trump has long criticized.

2026-07-01.title: Student Loan Repayment Shakeup Begins Today
2026-07-01.facts: Federal student loan borrowers are waking up to major changes in their repayment options starting today. Notices about the SAVE plan are going out in July, while other repayment programs are being phased out or newly launched.

2026-07-01.title: Anthropic Clears Export Hurdle Ahead of IPO
2026-07-01.facts: The U.S. government has lifted export controls on Anthropic's most advanced artificial intelligence models, clearing a major obstacle just as the company prepares for a crucial initial public offering.

2026-07-01.title: Nike Earnings Beat Masks Tariff Windfall
2026-07-01.facts: Nike crushed Wall Street's profit expectations this quarter, but there's a catch that investors need to understand. The athletic giant's earnings beat was significantly boosted by a one-time tariff refund, which padded both profit margins and gross margins beyond what the underlying business performance would suggest.

2026-07-01.title: New York's Neglected Buildings Don't Always Lose Money
2026-07-01.facts: Buildings with the worst housing code violations in New York actually turn a profit—just not from rent. A new report from the Rent Guidelines Board shows that landlords of buildings with high violation counts do collect less rent and have lower net operating income.

2026-07-01.title: Hamptons Summer Workers Face Brutal Housing Crunch
2026-07-01.facts: Seasonal restaurant workers in the Hamptons are sleeping on boats and bunking three to a bedroom as the housing crisis hits the service industry hard. Chefs, bartenders, and kitchen staff who make the summer scene possible are finding themselves priced out of even basic accommodations in one of America's priciest resort areas.

2026-07-01.title: Mbappé Closes in on Messi's World Cup Record
2026-07-01.facts: Kylian Mbappé is now just one goal away from Lionel Messi's all-time World Cup scoring record after leading France past Sweden 3-0 in the Round of 16. The French captain netted twice on Tuesday, breaking the record for most goals in World Cup knockout matches along the way.

2026-07-01.title: LeBron James Leaving Lakers
2026-07-01.facts: LeBron James is leaving the Lakers. The four-time NBA champion has informed the franchise that it can move on without him because he plans to play elsewhere, according to his agent Rich Paul, who spoke with ESPN on Tuesday.

2026-07-01.title: Claude Sonnet 5 and the Export Control Unlock
2026-07-01.facts: Anthropic dropped Claude Sonnet 5 today, and the headline isn't just that it's cheaper—it's that the Trump administration simultaneously lifted export controls on two previously restricted models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5. Sonnet 5 is being positioned as the new workhorse for agentic tasks, undercutting costs while keeping capabilities high enough for most autonomous workflows.

2026-07-02.title: Two-Year-Old Boy Pulled Alive From Venezuelan Earthquake Rubble After Six Days
2026-07-02.facts: Six days after twin earthquakes devastated Venezuela, rescue crews pulled a two-year-old boy alive from the rubble in what's being called a miracle. Kleiber Moran was found Wednesday, nearly a week after the quakes that have now killed at least 235 people and left hundreds more missing or trapped.

2026-07-02.title: China Says Pilot Crashed Plane Into Skyscraper to End His Life
2026-07-02.facts: Chinese authorities say a 66-year-old pilot deliberately crashed a small plane into a skyscraper for what they're calling "personal reasons," revealing he'd written about ending his life in his diary. The pilot, who died in the crash, had been suffering from anxiety, according to investigators who've now closed the case.

2026-07-02.title: Postal Worker Killed on North Carolina Route
2026-07-02.facts: A postal worker was killed while on her route in North Carolina, and a man's now facing murder and kidnapping charges in her death. Brandi Reynolds, 35, was working her delivery route when authorities say William Craig Durham, 56, abducted and killed her.

2026-07-02.title: Republican Senate Hopefuls Launch Independent Super PACs
2026-07-02.facts: Republican Senate candidates are breaking with tradition and launching their own super PACs instead of relying on the party's centralized fundraising machine in Washington. Most of the GOP's top contenders this cycle are setting up independent committees they control directly, bypassing the powerful Senate Leadership Fund that's traditionally handled big-money spending for the party.

2026-07-02.title: Trump Administration Declines to Extend North American Trade Deal
2026-07-02.facts: The Trump administration announced Wednesday it won't renew the USMCA trade agreement, setting a ten-year countdown to the deal's expiration. The decision stops short of scrapping the pact entirely but leaves American businesses in limbo about the future of trade with Canada and Mexico.

2026-07-02.title: A.I.'s Economic Impact Defies Measurement as Data Conflicts
2026-07-02.facts: Economists are struggling to figure out whether artificial intelligence is killing jobs or creating them, and the conflicting data isn't helping. Some employment statistics suggest A.I.

2026-07-02.title: Google Hit with €4.1 Billion Fine Over Android Practices
2026-07-02.facts: Google must pay a €4.1 billion fine for using its Android operating system to stifle competition, after losing its final appeal in European court. The ruling upholds the European Commission's finding that Google illegally forced smartphone makers to pre-install Google Search and Chrome as a condition of licensing the Android app store.

2026-07-02.title: Small-Cap Stocks Break Out on AI, Economy, and Biotech Surge
2026-07-02.facts: The Russell 2000 is finally having its moment. Strategists at Goldman Sachs say three key catalysts have powered the small-cap index's recent outperformance—and they're all flashing green at once.

2026-07-02.title: Bronx Gets $214 Million Senior Housing Development
2026-07-02.facts: The Bronx is about to see its largest transfer of affordable housing assistance on record. The Mamdani administration closed Wednesday on a $214 million development that'll bring 229 affordable senior housing units to the Morrisania neighborhood, with 80 of those units reserved specifically for residents of the nearby Morris Houses NYCHA complex.

2026-07-02.title: Flatiron Office Tower Sells for $28 Million
2026-07-02.facts: ZAR Property NY just paid $28 million for a 12-story office building in the Flatiron District, marking the largest commercial property sale to hit city records this week. The buyer picked up the 89,400-square-foot building at 118 West 22nd Street from GFP Real Estate, which had owned the property for decades.

2026-07-02.title: U.S. Men's Soccer Advances at World Cup
2026-07-02.facts: The U.S. men's national team is through to the World Cup round of 16 for the first time in more than two decades.

2026-07-02.title: Celtics and Sixers Pull Off Blockbuster Brown-George Swap
2026-07-02.facts: Boston just shook up the Eastern Conference in a big way. The Celtics have agreed to send Jaylen Brown to Philadelphia in exchange for Paul George, two first-round picks in 2028 and 2031, and two second-round selections.

2026-07-02.title: Anthropic Ships Claude Sonnet 5 and Unlocks Fable After Government Green Light
2026-07-02.facts: Anthropic just released Claude Sonnet 5, the newest version of its flagship AI model, and simultaneously the Trump administration lifted export restrictions that had kept two of the company's most powerful models—Fable 5 and Mythos—locked down for months. The restrictions were tied to concerns that Fable's advanced capabilities could be reverse-engineered or jailbroken by foreign competitors, particularly China.

2026-07-03.title: Venezuela Quake Survivor Found Alive After Eight Days
2026-07-03.facts: A man has been pulled alive from the rubble in Venezuela—eight days after the twin earthquakes that killed at least 920 people and left more than 51,000 missing. Hernán Gil was trapped under a collapsed multi-storey car park when rescuers finally reached him.

2026-07-03.title: Vietnam's Economic Boom Masks Deep Frustrations
2026-07-03.facts: High growth rates and surging exports have made Vietnam one of Asia's most ambitious economies—but those numbers don't tell the whole story. The country's building the world's largest stadium, a symbol of its global aspirations, yet deep frustrations simmer beneath the surface.

2026-07-03.title: Louisiana Attorney General Indicted on Malfeasance Charges
2026-07-03.facts: Louisiana's attorney general is facing criminal charges after allegedly bullying New Orleans city officials. Liz Murrill, a Republican, was indicted on counts of malfeasance and intimidation.

2026-07-03.title: UPS Didn't Instruct Inspectors to Check Failed Part in Fatal Crash
2026-07-03.facts: A critical breakdown in safety procedures preceded a deadly UPS cargo plane crash in Louisville. Inspectors weren't told to check a bearing that had already been flagged as faulty before the aircraft went down.

2026-07-03.title: FBI Expands Georgia Election Investigation at Trump's Direction
2026-07-03.facts: The FBI has assigned scores of analysts to investigate election records in Fulton County, Georgia, marking a significant expansion of an existing probe. The move reflects President Trump's continued push to validate his claims that the 2020 election in Georgia was rigged—claims that have been repeatedly debunked.

2026-07-03.title: June Jobs Report Shows Steady Growth, Eases Inflation Pressure
2026-07-03.facts: Employers added fewer jobs in June than they did a month earlier, but the unemployment rate ticked down to 4.2 percent—a decent showing that gives the Federal Reserve some breathing room. The labor market is clearly cooling from its red-hot pandemic recovery pace, but it's not collapsing.

2026-07-03.title: Trump Administration Secures $250 Million Donation from Micron
2026-07-03.facts: President Trump announced that chipmaker Micron will donate $250 million to a new type of investment account created by his administration. The move marks one of the largest corporate contributions to a government-backed initiative in recent memory.

2026-07-03.title: Britain's Workplace Sickness Crisis Targeted as Growth Strategy
2026-07-03.facts: More than 250 of Britain's biggest employers have signed up to a new taskforce aimed at tackling workplace sickness, which organizers say is holding back economic growth. The initiative, called 'Get Britain Working,' is being led by a former John Lewis boss who argues that getting people back to work after illness could unlock hidden productivity gains.

2026-07-03.title: Dow Hits Record Despite Weak Jobs Data
2026-07-03.facts: The Dow Jones Industrial Average notched a fresh record high even as Friday's jobs report came in weaker than expected, and Wall Street analysts say the rest of 2026 is going to be all about American workers. The disconnect between stock gains and employment weakness has strategists paying close attention to wage data, which showed little movement.

2026-07-03.title: Jury Convicts Long Island Man in $4.7 Million Harlem Deed Theft
2026-07-03.facts: A Long Island man is facing up to 25 years in prison after a jury found him guilty of stealing the deeds to two Harlem brownstones worth a combined $4.7 million. Joseph Makhani, a 63-year-old Kings Point resident, was convicted of deed theft at two properties: 107 West 118th Street and 135 West 131st Street.

2026-07-03.title: Rafael Cestero on What Comes After the NYC Rent Freeze
2026-07-03.facts: Now that Mayor Zohran Mamdani has delivered a rent freeze for nearly one million rent-stabilized apartments, the real estate industry is asking a blunt question: what happens to those buildings? Rafael Cestero is trying to answer that.

2026-07-03.title: Germany Fires Nagelsmann After World Cup Shock, Eyes Klopp
2026-07-03.facts: Julian Nagelsmann is out as Germany's head coach following the team's shock exit from the World Cup in the round of 32. The German Football Association wasted no time moving on from Nagelsmann, and talks are already underway with Jürgen Klopp to take over the national team.

2026-07-03.title: Lakers Reset Around Doncic as LeBron Departs
2026-07-03.facts: LeBron James announced Monday he's leaving the Lakers, and by Tuesday afternoon the entire franchise had pivoted to a new centerpiece: Luka Doncic. The overhaul happened in roughly half an hour, a breathtaking reset for a team that's spent the last several years built around James.

2026-07-03.title: Anthropic Slashes System Prompt by 80 Percent for Fable 5 Models
2026-07-03.facts: Anthropic's cut the system prompt for Claude Code by 80 percent with its new Fable 5 models, and the reason is kind of remarkable: the models themselves perform better with less instruction. It's a total reversal of the usual approach where you'd give a language model pages of context and rules to follow.

2026-07-04.title: Peru's Keiko Fujimori Declared Election Winner Nearly a Month After Vote
2026-07-04.facts: Keiko Fujimori has been declared the winner of Peru's presidential election nearly a month after voters went to the polls. The daughter of disgraced former president Alberto Fujimori prevailed in what's been described as a tight race, though the delay in announcing results has stretched weeks beyond what's typical.

2026-07-04.title: Albania's 'Flamingo Revolution' Erupts Over Luxury Development
2026-07-04.facts: Thousands of Albanians are flooding the streets demanding the prime minister's resignation, and they've adopted an unlikely symbol: flamingos. The protests—dubbed the "Flamingo Revolution"—were triggered by opposition to a luxury development project that critics say threatens the country's natural heritage.

2026-07-04.title: Wartime Fuel Shortages Bring Long Lines to Russian Gas Stations
2026-07-04.facts: Russians are lining up for hours at gas stations, shattering any remaining illusion that life at home has remained normal despite the war. "Are we in the Soviet Union now?" one frustrated Russian asked, captured in reports describing the chaos at the pumps.

2026-07-04.title: Millions Pack Tehran Streets for Ayatollah Khamenei's Funeral
2026-07-04.facts: Mourners have packed the streets of Tehran for the funeral of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, in what's shaping up to be a historic procession. Khamenei's body will lie in state at Tehran's Grand Mosalla until Monday before traveling across Iran and ultimately to Iraq for burial in Mashhad.

2026-07-04.title: Trump Memecoin Leaves Nearly a Million Investors With $3.8 Billion in Losses
2026-07-04.facts: Nearly a million investors have collectively lost $3.8 billion on the Trump memecoin, according to a new report from a cryptocurrency analytics firm. The analysis reveals a stark divide: most retail investors ended up in the red, while sophisticated traders managed to turn a profit.

2026-07-04.title: Representative Beatty Still Fighting After Kennedy Center Legal Win
2026-07-04.facts: Representative Joyce Beatty is still celebrating a legal victory over the Kennedy Center—and she's calling it a fight for justice. The Ohio Democrat sued after the president made changes to the performing arts center that she argued overstepped executive authority.

2026-07-04.title: Texas Hill Country Still Rebuilding One Year After Deadly Flood
2026-07-04.facts: It's been a year since catastrophic flooding killed 139 people in the Texas Hill Country, and communities along the Guadalupe River are still picking up the pieces. The flood, which struck last summer, was one of the deadliest natural disasters in Texas in recent memory.

2026-07-04.title: Beef Imports Surge, But Your Grill Bill Stays Sky-High
2026-07-04.facts: Record amounts of imported beef are pouring into the United States right now, yet anyone shopping for this weekend's Fourth of July barbecue knows prices are still painfully high. It's a puzzling disconnect that's frustrating consumers and highlighting a disconnect in how Washington thinks the beef market works.

2026-07-04.title: New 'Trump Accounts' Lock Kids' Savings Into U.S. Stocks Only
2026-07-04.facts: A new breed of children's investment account is making waves, and it comes with a very specific—and very risky—bet baked in. The so-called "Trump accounts," named after policy changes that encouraged their creation, ban two major asset classes outright: bonds and international stocks.

2026-07-04.title: Britain's Farm Labor Force Now Comes From Central Asia, Not Europe
2026-07-04.facts: A decade after Brexit, the workers picking strawberries in British fields look very different than they used to. Most seasonal agricultural laborers now come from Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan, a dramatic shift from the pre-Brexit era when EU citizens dominated the workforce.

2026-07-04.title: Nvidia Bets Big on Robotics Revolution
2026-07-04.facts: Nvidia's CEO Jensen Huang is calling humanoid robots a "multitrillion-dollar economic opportunity," and the chip giant is placing a serious wager on that future. The company's already pivoting resources toward what it sees as the next frontier after AI—robots that can walk, work, and navigate the physical world.

2026-07-04.title: NYC Rent Freeze Spurs Lender Questions on Stabilized Building Future
2026-07-04.facts: Rafael Cestero's phone has been ringing nonstop since Mayor Mamdani's rent freeze took effect on nearly a million rent-stabilized apartments across New York City. Cestero runs the Community Preservation Corporation, the nation's largest federally certified lender focused on multifamily housing in low-income communities, and he's fielding anxious questions from building owners who now face frozen income streams.

2026-07-04.title: Chapman Becomes All-Time Strikeout King for Relievers
2026-07-04.facts: Aroldis Chapman just carved his name into baseball's record books. The Red Sox lefty passed Hoyt Wilhelm on Friday night to become the all-time leader in strikeouts by a reliever, reaching 1,364 for his career.

2026-07-04.title: LeBron James Exploring Move as Multiple Teams Enter Pursuit
2026-07-04.facts: LeBron James is on the move again. His agent, Rich Paul, confirmed that James is actively considering several franchises for his next destination, with the Heat, Cavaliers, and 76ers all in the mix.

2026-07-04.title: Lakers Ship Ayton to Washington in Salary-Clearing Deal
2026-07-04.facts: The Lakers are trading center Deandre Ayton to the Wizards in exchange for guard Jaden Hardy and two future second-round picks. It's a clear salary dump for Los Angeles, moving Ayton's substantial contract off their books as they chase bigger moves in free agency.

2026-07-04.title: Anthropic Publishes Technical Details on Fable 5 Security After Subscription Controversy
2026-07-04.facts: Anthropic just released a technical deep-dive into the cybersecurity safeguards built into Claude Fable 5, and it's paired with something equally noteworthy: a new framework for testing jailbreaks. The company's being unusually transparent about how it fortified its most powerful model against misuse, publishing details on the specific controls that prevent Fable 5 from being coaxed into generating malicious code or bypassing safety filters.

2026-07-05.title: Venezuela Housing Warnings Went Unheeded Before Deadly Quakes
2026-07-05.facts: Residents, construction experts, and seismologists had been sounding alarms for years about Venezuela's public housing—and now their worst fears have come to pass. The buildings that collapsed in last week's earthquake had been flagged as vulnerable to natural disasters long before the ground started shaking.

2026-07-05.title: Philippines Moves to Impeach Vice President Sara Duterte
2026-07-05.facts: The impeachment trial of Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte is set to begin, a dramatic escalation in the country's political crisis. Duterte, who has said she plans to run for president, faces allegations of corruption and charges that she made death threats against President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.

2026-07-05.title: Trump Administration Rolls Back Dozens of Gun Regulations
2026-07-05.facts: The Trump administration has dismantled dozens of federal gun regulations, marking one of the most significant rollbacks of firearm oversight in recent memory. The changes reverse rules put in place during the Biden presidency, essentially returning gun regulations to where they stood before 2021.

2026-07-05.title: Typhoon Bavi Bears Down on U.S. Pacific Territories
2026-07-05.facts: Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands are bracing for a direct hit from Typhoon Bavi, a massive tropical cyclone churning through the Pacific with the force of a Category 5 hurricane. The storm is forecast to pass near the U.S.

2026-07-05.title: Yosemite Overwhelmed After Trump Administration Ends Reservation System
2026-07-05.facts: Yosemite National Park is drowning in its own popularity this summer, with visitors enduring exceptionally long waits after the Trump administration eliminated a reservation system that had controlled access during peak season. The decision has turned one of America's most iconic parks into a bottleneck of traffic jams, packed trails, and overwhelmed facilities.

2026-07-05.title: June Jobs Report Signals Bond Market Opportunity
2026-07-05.facts: June's jobs report is weaker than the headline numbers suggest, and bond investors are taking notice. The latest employment data shows enough softness to make fixed-income markets look increasingly attractive, even as stock investors focus on other metrics.

2026-07-05.title: Federal Rule Shifts Roof Replacement Costs to Homeowners
2026-07-05.facts: A new federal insurance rule is forcing homeowners to shoulder more of the cost when it's time to replace a roof—and the timing couldn't be worse with hail and hurricane season underway. The regulation has allowed insurers to shift a bigger portion of roof-replacement expenses onto policyholders, leaving many facing a tough choice when storms hit.

2026-07-05.title: Ukraine's Drone Campaign Triggers Russia's Worst Fuel Crisis in Decades
2026-07-05.facts: Ukraine is striking Russian energy infrastructure at an unprecedented pace, and it's starting to show. Kyiv's intensified drone campaign has hit so many refineries, pipelines, and storage facilities that Russia is now facing its worst fuel crisis in decades.

2026-07-05.title: Trump Account Investment Rules Unveiled
2026-07-05.facts: Parents who received money in so-called "Trump accounts" for their children now know exactly where they can invest it. The Treasury Department just released guidance specifying that the funds must be placed in low-cost index funds, and they've published a list of eligible options families can choose from.

2026-07-05.title: The Cromans Return to Manhattan Multifamily
2026-07-05.facts: Jake and Adam Croman are quietly rebuilding the family name in New York real estate—and they're doing it by betting on exactly the kind of buildings most investors won't touch. The brothers have been assembling a portfolio of small Manhattan apartment buildings, focusing heavily on rent-stabilized assets that the broader market has avoided since the 2019 rent law overhaul made value-add plays far less profitable.

2026-07-05.title: Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift Tie the Knot at Madison Square Garden
2026-07-05.facts: Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift got married Friday night at Madison Square Garden in what's being described as one of the year's most star-studded ceremonies. The Kansas City Chiefs tight end and the pop superstar said their vows in front of a celebrity-packed guest list at the iconic New York venue.

2026-07-05.title: 76ers Land Jaylen Brown, Make Push for LeBron
2026-07-05.facts: The 76ers pulled off one of the biggest moves of the offseason by acquiring Jaylen Brown in a trade—and they're not done yet. Just one day after landing Brown, Philadelphia has entered the hunt for LeBron James and is actively trying to convince him to join the team, according to league sources.

2026-07-05.title: Lakers Ship Deandre Ayton to Washington in Surprise Deal
2026-07-05.facts: The Lakers are sending center Deandre Ayton to the Washington Wizards in exchange for guard Jaden Hardy and two future second-round picks. It's a move that clears significant salary for Los Angeles while giving Washington some much-needed size in the middle.

2026-07-05.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Opus 4.7
2026-07-05.facts: Anthropic just dropped Claude Opus 4.7, the latest iteration of its flagship reasoning model—and the performance benchmarks are making waves. The new release pushes improvements in what Anthropic calls "extended thinking," letting the model spend more compute on harder problems before delivering an answer.

2026-07-06.title: Venezuela Earthquake Response Reveals New U.S. Aid Strategy
2026-07-06.facts: The Trump administration's approach to the Venezuela earthquake crisis looks nothing like the massive relief operation the U.S. mounted for Haiti after its 2010 disaster.

2026-07-06.title: French Court to Rule on Marine Le Pen's Presidential Eligibility
2026-07-06.facts: Marine Le Pen's political future hangs in the balance as a French appeals court prepares to deliver its verdict on whether she can run for president in 2027. The leader of France's National Rally party is currently appealing a conviction that could bar her from holding public office.

2026-07-06.title: China Fires Submarine Missile Into Pacific, Rattling Neighbors
2026-07-06.facts: China test-fired a long-range ballistic missile from a submarine into the Pacific Ocean, sending a clear signal of its growing military reach just as Australia locked down new defense agreements with Pacific Island nations. The launch marks a rare public demonstration of China's submarine-launched missile capabilities, a key component of its nuclear deterrent.

2026-07-06.title: Iran Mourns Khamenei as Calls for Retaliation Intensify
2026-07-06.facts: Enormous crowds packed the streets of Tehran for a third straight day as Iran held funeral processions for Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, with mourners calling for revenge against the United States and Israel. The scale of the gatherings reflects both the leader's decades-long grip on power and the state's ability to mobilize massive public displays of grief and anger.

2026-07-06.title: Research Universities Slash Ph.D. Programs Amid Federal Funding Uncertainty
2026-07-06.facts: Some of America's top research universities are shrinking their doctoral programs, and it's a troubling sign for the future of American science. The cuts are being driven by uncertainty over federal funding, which has traditionally been the backbone of graduate research support.

2026-07-06.title: Trump Appoints Former Defense Attorney to Federal Bench
2026-07-06.facts: Emil Bove defended the president in court, and now he's getting a lifetime appointment as a federal judge. President Trump announced he's nominating Bove to the federal bench, continuing a pattern of appointing his former personal lawyers to judgeships.

2026-07-06.title: Prosecutors Detail Murder Case in Charlie Kirk Killing
2026-07-06.facts: A preliminary hearing is underway today in the murder case that shocked the country—the killing of Charlie Kirk. Prosecutors will lay out their evidence against the accused killer as a judge decides whether there's enough to proceed to trial.

2026-07-06.title: Samsung Eyes Major Price Hike as Chip Shortage Tightens
2026-07-06.facts: Samsung Electronics is reportedly planning to raise prices on its memory chips by 20%, a move that signals just how tight the global semiconductor market has become. The proposed increase would hit dynamic random access memory chips—the kind that go into everything from smartphones to data centers.

2026-07-06.title: Oil's Retreat From Danger Zone Could Boost Stocks
2026-07-06.facts: Oil prices have pulled back sharply from their recent highs, and Wall Street analysts say that's good news for the broader economy. Evercore ISI points to historical patterns suggesting that when oil exits what they call the "danger zone," stocks tend to rally.

2026-07-06.title: EasyJet Takeover Deal Faces Market Skepticism
2026-07-06.facts: EasyJet says it's reached an agreement in principle on a £5.2 billion takeover by U.S. investment firm Castlelake, but the market isn't exactly celebrating.

2026-07-06.title: Manhattan's Ultra-Luxury Condo Market Hits Decade High
2026-07-06.facts: Manhattan's new development market just posted its strongest quarter in years—but only if you're shopping above $10 million. Contracts signed for new condos asking $10 million or more nearly doubled in the second quarter compared to last year, jumping from 21 to 38 deals, according to Brown Harris Stevens Development Marketing.

2026-07-06.title: Florida Closes Live Local Loophole That Protected Cities From Lawsuits
2026-07-06.facts: Florida just made it harder for cities to push back against affordable housing developments—and buried the change deep in a bill that Governor Ron DeSantis recently signed. House Bill 1389 expands the definition of a "person" under the state's fair housing law to include agencies, government entities, and other legal or commercial entities, a shift specifically aimed at discouraging local governments from challenging Live Local Act applications.

2026-07-06.title: England Survives Thriller to Knock Mexico Out of World Cup
2026-07-06.facts: England's scraped through to the World Cup quarterfinals with a nerve-shredding 3-2 victory over Mexico in the round of 16. The match was anything but smooth—England found themselves navigating a rollercoaster that included three major VAR incidents, according to a breakdown of the controversial calls.

2026-07-06.title: Lakers Ship Ayton to Washington in Center Shakeup
2026-07-06.facts: The Lakers are sending center Deandre Ayton to the Wizards in exchange for guard Jaden Hardy and two future second-round picks. It's a significant move for Los Angeles as they continue reshaping their roster heading into next season.

2026-07-06.title: Patrick Ewing Joins Wizards Coaching Staff
2026-07-06.facts: Patrick Ewing is heading back to the NBA bench. The Georgetown and Knicks legend has agreed to join the Wizards as an assistant coach under head coach Brian Keefe.

2026-07-06.title: Claude Code Hooks Let You Build Guardrails for Autonomous Agents
2026-07-06.facts: You're already running scheduled tasks with Claude Code, but here's the pattern most people miss: the four hooks—PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, and SessionStart—give you precise interception points to build guardrails around autonomous runs without touching the main prompt. PreToolUse fires right before Claude calls a tool, so you can log the intent, check parameters against a whitelist, or block high-risk actions like file deletions or API calls to production endpoints.
